name: vscode-safe-exec
description: Best-effort approval, rollback review, and recovery for risky terminal commands, explicit VS Code command wrappers, suspicious text edits, and supported file operations.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Safe Exec can still show approvals, roll back suspicious text edits, and observe supported file-operation events in untrusted workspaces where VS Code allows it. Workspace Trust is not a sandbox, and command coverage still depends on explicit Safe Exec proxy or wrapper commands.

commands.title: Safe Exec: Open Recommended Proxy And Wrapper Keybindings
Safe Exec: Show Recent File Operations
Safe Exec: Restore Last Recoverable File Operation
Safe Exec: Browse Recoverable File Operations
Safe Exec: Proxy Run Selected Text In Terminal
Safe Exec: Proxy Run All Notebook Cells
Safe Exec: Proxy Execute Notebook Cell
Safe Exec: Proxy Execute Interactive Input
Safe Exec: Proxy GitHub Copilot Generate

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Safe Exec
walkthroughs.description: Review the first-run guidance, wire explicit proxy keybindings, and inspect recent Safe Exec activity and file-operation history.

steps.title: Review the onboarding guide
steps.description: Open the onboarding guide to see what Safe Exec covers, where coverage stops, and how Workspace Trust fits in.

steps.title: Review recommended proxy and wrapper keybindings
steps.description: Open your user keybindings.json beside recommended Safe Exec proxy and wrapper snippets. Safe Exec does not edit keybindings automatically.

media.altText: Illustrative Safe Exec status and keybinding guidance

steps.title: Inspect the diff-based edit review flow
steps.description: Safe Exec rolls back suspicious edits first, then lets you review a real diff before reapplying.

steps.title: Open recent Safe Exec activity
steps.description: Review approvals, denials, degraded replays, file-operation recovery events, and related best-effort status history for this workspace.

steps.title: Review recoverable file operations
steps.description: Open recent best-effort file-operation history and restore options for supported delete and rename flows. Safe Exec records these flows but does not approval-gate them.

media.altText: Illustrative Safe Exec file operation recovery

safeExec.enabled.description: Enable Safe Exec's best-effort approval prompts, edit rollback review, and supported file-operation handling.

safeExec.rulesPath.description: Workspace-relative or absolute path to the JSON rules file.

enumDescriptions: Protect package publishing, deploy-style scripts, env files, and JS toolchain config.
Protect packaging, lockfiles, Python env configuration, and dependency sync commands.
Protect container/image cleanup, compose changes, and Docker build configuration.
Protect infrastructure apply/change commands and IaC manifests.
Protect force-pushes, workflow triggers, and CI pipeline configuration.
Protect destructive disk and volume tooling, partition rewrites, and storage administration surfaces.
Protect service/task persistence, shell profile mutation, and autorun configuration across platforms.
Protect credential and identity files, secret-bearing key material, and outbound upload-style commands.
Protect registry publish flows, container pushes, release tooling, and common cloud deploy commands.

safeExec.policyBundles.description: Opt-in Safe Exec policy bundle IDs. Built-in bundles add stack- and workflow-specific command, path, and sensitive-file rules; they do not create transparent interception.

safeExec.protectedCommands.description: Automation-heavy command IDs that should require approval when executed through Safe Exec proxy or wrapper commands.

safeExec.terminal.killStrategy.description: How Safe Exec attempts to stop a risky terminal command after shell integration reports it.

enumDescriptions: Use the current best-effort behavior for critical commands, including sendText fallback when shell-integration replay is unavailable.
Replay critical commands automatically only through shell integration. If the original stop is unconfirmed or shell integration is unavailable, fall back to manual replay.
Never auto-replay critical commands. After approval, open a fresh terminal and copy the command to the clipboard for manual replay.
Deny critical command replay when Safe Exec cannot confirm the original terminal stopped. If shell integration is unavailable after a confirmed stop, fall back to manual replay.
safeExec.terminal.criticalReplayPolicy.description: How Safe Exec handles approved critical terminal commands when replay fidelity is reduced.

safeExec.editHeuristics.minChangedCharac.description: Minimum changed characters before Safe Exec treats a post-change edit as suspicious, rolls it back, and asks whether to reapply it.

safeExec.editHeuristics.minAffectedLines.description: Minimum affected lines before Safe Exec treats a post-change edit as suspicious, rolls it back, and asks whether to reapply it.

safeExec.editHeuristics.maxPreviewCharac.description: Legacy fallback preview size for suspicious edit review.
safeExec.editHeuristics.maxPreviewCharac.deprecationMessage: Safe Exec now uses a full diff view for suspicious edit review. This setting is retained for compatibility and no longer affects the primary review flow.

safeExec.fileOps.enabled.description: Enable Safe Exec best-effort file-operation observation, classification, and bounded recovery for supported VS Code create, delete, and rename events.

safeExec.fileOps.maxSnapshotBytes.description: Maximum bytes Safe Exec will capture per file snapshot before delete or rename. Larger files fall back to metadata-only recovery.

safeExec.fileOps.maxFilesPerOperation.description: Maximum files Safe Exec will snapshot for a single delete or rename operation before falling back to partial or metadata-only recovery.

safeExec.fileOps.minBulkOperationCount.description: Minimum affected file count before Safe Exec classifies a file operation as bulk.

safeExec.fileOps.protectedPathPatterns.description: Additional regex path patterns that should raise file-operation risk and snapshot priority.

safeExec.fileOps.ignoredPathPatterns.description: Additional regex path patterns Safe Exec should treat as lower-signal when evaluating file operations.

safeExec.fileOps.sensitiveExtensions.description: Additional file extensions that should raise file-operation risk and recovery priority.

safeExec.fileOps.sensitiveFileNames.description: Additional file names that should raise file-operation risk and recovery priority.

safeExec.fileOps.captureBinarySnapshots.description: Allow Safe Exec to capture byte-for-byte snapshots for small binary files. When disabled, binary files fall back to metadata-only recovery.

scripts.test: npm run compile && npm run test:run
scripts.coverage: npm run compile && npm run coverage:run
scripts.check: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run compile && npm run test:run && npm run coverage:run
